title: salix × scandica
intro: Salix × scandica, a hybrid of the willow genus, is a deciduous shrub or small tree known for its slender branches and fragrant catkins. Traditionally, the bark of Salix × scandica has been used to create willow bark extract, which contains salicin, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a natural remedy for pain relief and reducing fever. The medicinal properties of Salix × scandica have been used to alleviate menstrual cramps, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions, offering health benefits such as reduced pain and improved mobility. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
